senses.definition: plural of reinsurer, a person or company that provides insurance for other insurers by taking on part of their risk
examples: Several reinsurers are evaluating the terms of the treaty.
The reinsurers agreed to share a portion of the catastrophe risk.

senses.definition: to insure again or to insure something with a reinsurer; to transfer risk to a reinsurer
examples: The insurer reinsures part of the policy with another company.
They reinsure the large commercial risk to spread the potential loss.

senses.definition: present participle of reinsure; the act of transferring risk to a reinsurer
examples: The company is reinsuring its offshore exposure.
Reinsuring helps stabilize losses from large claims.

senses.definition: to bring someone or something back into a group, system, or organization after being separated
examples: The program aims to reintegrate former prisoners into society.
Efforts to reintegrate families after the lockdown continued.

senses.definition: having been brought back into a group, system, or society after separation
examples: The reintegrated veterans reported better mental health.
A reintegrated habitat supports returning species.

senses.definition: to bring someone or something back into a group or system
examples: The program reintegrates ex-offenders into the community.
New policies reintegrate outdated practices with modern methods.

senses.definition: present participle of reintegrate; the process of bringing back into a group or system
examples: Efforts are reintegrating refugees into local communities.
They are reintegrating the software modules into the main system.

senses.definition: the act or process of returning to a group or system after being separated
examples: The reintegration of ex-prisoners into society is challenging.
Reintegration programs help with adjustment after relocation.

senses.definition: plural of reintegration; instances or acts of reintegration
examples: The report covers several reintegrations of displaced families.
Policy changes will affect multiple reintegrations over time.

senses.definition: related to or characterized by reintegration; helping to reintegrate
examples: Reintegrative programs focus on social support and employment.
They explored reintegrative approaches to housing policy.

senses.definition: to intend again or anew; to have a different or revised intention
examples: They reintend their project to focus on sustainability.
If plans fail, they may reintend their approach.

senses.definition: to enter again or to insert again; very rare usage
examples: They sought to reinter the data into the dataset.

senses.definition: to intervene on behalf of someone; to intercede again
examples: They hoped to reintercede with the judge.
Parents may reintercede to settle a dispute.

senses.definition: the act of interceding on someone’s behalf; a second or renewed intercession
examples: Her reintercession helped avert a dispute.
The council’s reintercession was appreciated by all parties.

senses.definition: to interchange again or to swap back
examples: They needed to reinterchange the component connections.
The parts were reinterchanged to restore compatibility.

senses.definition: to interest again; to regain attention or curiosity
examples: A new feature reinterested users in the app.
The campaign reinterested the community in environmental issues.

senses.definition: to interfere again; to meddle again
examples: They warned others not to reinterfere in the dispute.
Without consent, he should not reinterfere with the process.

senses.definition: the act of interfering again; renewed meddling
examples: The report criticized reinterference in technical decisions.
Reinterference can undermine project autonomy.

senses.definition: the act of burying a body again in a different place
examples: The reinterment of the remains took place in the family burial ground.
A ceremonial reinterment was held for the soldiers.

senses.definition: plural of reinterment; multiple acts of reburial
examples: The cemetery documented several reinterments over the years.
Policies regulate multiple reinterments in the region.

senses.definition: to interpret something in a new or different way
examples: The critic reinterprets the novel as a satire.
Scientists reinterpret the data to fit new models.

senses.definition: a new or different interpretation of something
examples: The reinterpretation of the text changed its meaning.
Historical reinterpretations can shift our understanding of events.

senses.definition: plural of reinterpretation; multiple instances of new interpretations
examples: The book offers several reinterpretations of the myth.
Scholars proposed different reinterpretations in the symposium.

senses.definition: having been interpreted in a new or different way
examples: The reinterpreted passage surprised many readers.
They presented a reinterpreted version of the event.

senses.definition: present participle of reinterpret; the act of interpreting anew
examples: Experts are reinterpreting the fossil evidence.
They are reinterpreting the results in light of new data.
